item.title: Muna's Lessons from the Veranda
item.description: June 2026 Adisa Ancestry Artists Residency, Jamaica Listen Listen To the birds. They say There’s nowhere else to be. Listen To the trees. They find Stillness without ease. Listen To the bees. They hum With life-force energy Listen To the flowers. They are Perfection without performance. Listen To the green. It sustains The beating heart of our world. About The Poem “Listen” came to me while sitting on the veranda of the Adisa Ancestry Artists Residency in Jamaica. This ...
item.dc:creator: Muna Obiefule

item.title: Hot Girl Spring…but it’s too Hot to Play
item.description: April 2026 Girl, it’s too hot and it's only getting worse. Spring isn’t supposed to feel like this. But across the United States, temperatures are already climbing past seasonal norms and according to NOAA Climate Prediction Center , spring 2026 is shaping up to be unusually warm, with “ heat dome ” conditions looming in many regions. And as always, the heat won’t hit everyone the same. Communities of color are expected to bear the brunt of these rising temperatures, facing higher exposure...
item.dc:creator: Danait Issac

item.title: Dear Everything
item.description: Last fall, GPE was invited to be a part of the Atlanta Youth Council for Dear Everything: A Musical Uprising for the Earth . Dear Everything is a climate musical created by V (formerly Eve Ensler), the world-acclaimed playwright and activist best known for The Vagina Monologues. Dear Everything follows a group of adults in a small town willing to sacrifice their forest for money, and the young people, led by Sophia, a teenage girl, whose determination to save the forest endows them with...
item.dc:creator: Danait Issac

item.title: What’s the status of Data Centers in your city?
item.description: March 2026 Back in December, we released a special member blog about SZA x data centers that received a lot of traction around the expansion of data centers in Memphis, TN. We’ve decided to spin the block and provide a more comprehensive update on the impact of data centers in another one of our major member states: Georgia . Plus for our girlies everywhere else, we’ll plug resources on where you can find more information about data centers local to you! But first let’s take a couple steps...
item.dc:creator: Danait Issac

item.title: Breaking up with your Microplastics
item.description: February 2026 With Valentine’s Day right around the corner, it’s impossible to ignore how powerless we’ve all become to microplastics slowly creeping into every single organ of our bodies including our hearts. Though we love a good slow burn (anyone watching the new season of Bridgerton #Beneophie ‍♀️), don’t be fooled! These pesky little particles are more troublesome than you can imagine. From respiratory issues to disruptions in the endocrine system, check out our previous blog post...
item.dc:creator: Danait Issac

item.title: When Beauty Innovation Centers Black Women & the Planet‍♀️
item.description: January 2026 As we step into a new year, many of us are rethinking not just our routines, but the products we use and the values behind them. For Aubrey Patton, that reflection led to the creation of AKYN Beauty , a brand rooted in sustainability, innovation, and care for Black women and the planet. AKYN Beauty was born from a simple but deeply familiar problem: makeup stains. For so many women, especially Black women who wear deeper, melanated foundation shades, stains on collars, dresses,...
item.dc:creator: Aubrey Patton

item.title: Rhythm, Blues, and Toxic Fumes: Guess Who’s Coming to Pollute?
item.description: December 2025 Data centers are on everyone’s minds lately, and for good reason. They have quickly become a unifying issue as communities across the country push back against the growing demand for new construction. And all too often, these facilities end up in predominantly low-income Black and Brown neighborhoods, where residents are already carrying the weight of environmental harm. Even R&B singer SZA recently spoke out against a proposed data center in Memphis. Keep reading to hear a...
item.dc:creator: Bilen Gimikael

item.title: We’ve Got the Receipts. Here’s How You Can Join the Movement!
item.description: November 2025 2024-25 has been a huge year for Girl Plus Environment . From training young leaders, to amplifying environmental justice campaigns , GPE has been building the tools and infrastructure for Black and Brown women+ to shape their own environmental solutions. In this post, we’re celebrating how our members took action and sharing how you can deepen your involvement through membership, donations, and spreading the word. This year, GPE has created, launched, and completed three...
item.dc:creator: Danait Issac

item.description: October 2025 Inspired by The Vagina Monologues , a play turned global phenomenon that gave voice to women’s lived experiences and...
item.dc:creator: Danait Issac

item.description: September 2025 Dear Diary, I can’t believe it’s been a full orbit around the sun since I stepped into the role of Community Manager at...

item.title: Self Care with An Environmental Twist: How Nia Wilson Is Making Sustainable Wellness Accessible to Her Community
item.description: At just 23, Nia Wilson is already shifting the conversation around self-care with her brand, The Natural Elixir, LLC. Focused on clean,...
